title: Medical Examiner Rules Both Minneapolis ICE Shootings Homicides
excerpt: The Hennepin County Medical Examiner ruled the deaths of both Alex Pretti and Renee Good homicides, formally classifying both killings as caused by another person. The rulings intensified calls for independent investigation as the federal government continued to block state investigators from evidence.
content:
On February 2, 2026, the Hennepin County Medical Examiner's Office released autopsy findings ruling the deaths of both Alex Pretti (shot January 24) and Renee Good (shot January 7) as homicides.

  • Pretti's cause of death was listed as 'multiple gunshot wounds,' noting he was 'shot by law enforcement officer(s).'
  • Good's January 7 death was similarly ruled a homicide by the same office.
  • The homicide classification means both deaths were caused by another person; it does not automatically indicate criminal conduct, but it created legal pressure for formal investigations.
  • The Department of Homeland Security announced that all federal agents in Operation Metro Surge would be issued body cameras following the rulings.
  • The Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension stated the FBI was refusing to share evidence and information related to the shootings.
  • Federal civil rights investigations were subsequently announced, but Minnesota state officials said they were being frozen out of the process entirely.
'There has to be an investigation any time a federal agent or a state agent takes the life of a person in our community.' — Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty
source: KTVU FOX 2 / Hennepin County Medical Examiner
